scope_boundary: Deterministic public-safe workflow-trace accounting only: three selected routes, fixture totals, expected-invalid controls, and a Lean/Python trace-property bridge. No deployed scheduler, route-search completeness, physical feasibility, model quality, economic outcome, or chapter-core support movement is established.
transition_reason: The workflow trace is valuable because it checks selected-route cost recomputation, high-risk-first ordering, protected review, verification minutes, displaced-cost residual ownership, over-budget rejection, latency-only rejection, and support-state non-promotion. It remains a deterministic fixture bridge, so it blocks promotion for any claim that would require live scheduler behavior or external workload review.
required_artifacts: live or externally reviewed scheduler trace
workload-quality review beyond deterministic fixture fields
independent review before broader support movement
negative_results: over-budget aggregate resource bills are rejected
low-risk work scheduled before protected high-risk review is rejected
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: Lean bridge drift from the result record
chapter prose treats the trace as deployed scheduler evidence
support-state promotion without a separate accepted transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed workflow-trace claim after live or externally reviewed scheduler traces, route-quality review, measured residual/displaced-cost accounting, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted transition.
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent scheduler, economics, workload-quality, or source-interpretation audit
non_claims: does not promote Resource Economics above argument
does not prove deployed scheduler behavior
does not prove model quality, economic outcomes, physical feasibility, or route-search completeness
does not create an upward support-state transition
